summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened a new five-week series on the Book of James, a letter he described as both practical and powerful for everyday living. He introduced James as the younger brother of Jesus, who became a believer after the resurrection and a key leader in the early church. The sermon focused on the first chapter of James, breaking it down into three key highlights. First, Pastor Travis taught that believers are to 'count it all joy' when facing trials, because God uses these tests of faith to produce perseverance, maturity, and refinement, ultimately bringing us out 'as gold.' He shared a personal testimony of speaking this verse during a major health crisis as evidence of God's sustaining power. Second, he addressed triumph over temptation, clarifying that being tempted is not a sin, but yielding to it is. He explained the progression from desire to sin and emphasized that God always provides a way out through His Word, prayer, the Holy Spirit, and accountability. Finally, Pastor Travis stressed the importance of not merely hearing God's word but doing it, warning against self-deception and calling for a faith that walks in obedience. He concluded with an invitation for those ready to begin building their lives on the rock of Christ's teachings.
mainPoints: Believers are to count it all joy in trials, trusting that God uses them to produce perseverance and spiritual maturity.
Temptation is not a sin, but yielding to it is; God provides a way out through His Word, prayer, the Spirit, and accountability.
True faith requires action; we must not only hear God's word but do it to avoid self-deception and receive blessing.

versesMentioned.text: For even his own brothers did not believe in him.

For what I received I passed on to you as of first importance: that Christ died for our sins according to the Scriptures, that he was buried, that he was raised on the third day according to the Scriptures, and that he appeared to Cephas, and then to the Twelve. After that, he appeared to more than five hundred of the brothers and sisters at the same time, most of whom are still living, though some have fallen asleep. Then he appeared to James, then to all the apostles, and last of all he appeared to me also, as to one abnormally born.
James, a servant of God and of the Lord Jesus Christ, To the twelve tribes scattered among the nations: Greetings.

Consider it pure joy, my brothers and sisters, whenever you face trials of many kinds, because you know that the testing of your faith produces perseverance. Let perseverance finish its work so that you may be mature and complete, not lacking anything.

If any of you lacks wisdom, you should ask God, who gives generously to all without finding fault, and it will be given to you. But when you ask, you must believe and not doubt, because the one who doubts is like a wave of the sea, blown and tossed by the wind. That person should not expect to receive anything from the Lord. Such a person is double-minded and unstable in all they do.
He causes his sun to rise on the evil and the good, and sends rain on the righteous and the unrighteous.
But he knows the way that I take; when he has tested me, I will come forth as gold.
See, I have refined you, though not as silver; I have tested you in the furnace of affliction.

These have come so that the proven genuineness of your faith—of greater worth than gold, which perishes even though refined by fire—may result in praise, glory and honor when Jesus Christ is revealed.
Blessed is the one who perseveres under trial because, having stood the test, that person will receive the crown of life that the Lord has promised to those who love him.

When tempted, no one should say, “God is tempting me.” For God cannot be tempted by evil, nor does he tempt anyone; but each person is tempted when they are dragged away by their own evil desire and enticed. Then, after desire has conceived, it gives birth to sin; and sin, when it is full-grown, gives birth to death.
For we do not have a high priest who is unable to empathize with our weaknesses, but we have one who has been tempted in every way, just as we are—yet he did not sin.

For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have eternal life. For God did not send his Son into the world to condemn the world, but to save the world through him.
For all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God.
For the wages of sin is death, but the gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.

No temptation has overtaken you except what is common to mankind. And God is faithful; he will not let you be tempted beyond what you can bear. But when you are tempted, he will also provide a way out so that you can endure it.
I have hidden your word in my heart that I might not sin against you.
Watch and pray so that you will not fall into temptation. The spirit is willing, but the flesh is weak.
So I say, walk by the Spirit, and you will not gratify the desires of the flesh.
Submit yourselves, then, to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you.
Therefore confess your sins to each other and pray for each other so that you may be healed. The prayer of a righteous person is powerful and effective.

My dear brothers and sisters, take note of this: Everyone should be quick to listen, slow to speak and slow to become angry, because human anger does not produce the righteousness that God desires. Therefore, get rid of all moral filth and the evil that is so prevalent and humbly accept the word planted in you, which can save you.
To answer before listening— that is folly and shame.
Sin is not ended by multiplying words, but the prudent hold their tongues.

Do not merely listen to the word, and so deceive yourselves. Do what it says. Anyone who listens to the word but does not do what it says is like someone who looks at his face in a mirror and, after looking at himself, goes away and immediately forgets what he looks like. But whoever looks intently into the perfect law that gives freedom, and continues in it—not forgetting what they have heard, but doing it—they will be blessed in what they do.

Therefore everyone who hears these words of mine and puts them into practice is like a wise man who built his house on the rock. The rain came down, the streams rose, and the winds blew and beat against that house; yet it did not fall, because it had its foundation on the rock. But everyone who hears these words of mine and does not put them into practice is like a foolish man who built his house on sand. The rain came down, the streams rose, and the winds blew and beat against that house, and it fell with a great crash.
If you love me, keep my commands.

Monday.scripture: James 1:2-4 (NIV) - 'Consider it pure joy...whenever you face trials of many kinds, because you know that the testing of your faith produces perseverance.'
Monday.focus: Finding joy in God's purpose for your current trial.
Monday.action: Identify one difficult situation you are facing. Write down one way God might be using it to build perseverance in you.
Monday.prayer: Lord, help me to see my trials through Your eyes today. Give me the faith to consider it joy, trusting that You are at work to mature me. Amen.

Tuesday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 10:13 (NIV) - 'God is faithful; he will not let you be tempted beyond what you can bear. But when you are tempted, he will also provide a way out.'
Tuesday.action: Think of one recurring temptation. Identify one practical 'way out' God has provided (e.g., a scripture, a person to call, a change of scenery).
Tuesday.prayer: Faithful God, thank You that You always provide an escape. Open my eyes to see the way out today and give me the strength to take it. Amen.

Wednesday.scripture: James 1:22 (NIV) - 'Do not merely listen to the word, and so deceive yourselves. Do what it says.'
Wednesday.action: Recall one clear instruction from Scripture you heard recently. Take one concrete step today to obey it.
Wednesday.prayer: Holy Spirit, convict me where I am only a hearer. Empower me to be a doer of Your word in a specific way today. Amen.

Thursday.scripture: Job 23:10 (NIV) - 'But he knows the way that I take; when he has tested me, I will come forth as gold.'
Thursday.action: Reflect on a past trial. Write down one way you grew or were strengthened through it, seeing how God brought 'gold' out of it.
Thursday.prayer: Refiner, I trust that You know my path completely. Help me to endure current tests with hope, believing I will come out more valuable and pure for Your glory. Amen.

Friday.scripture: James 1:19 (NIV) - 'Everyone should be quick to listen, slow to speak and slow to become angry.'
Friday.action: In one conversation today, make a conscious effort to listen fully before you respond. Pause before speaking.
Friday.prayer: God of patience, guard my mouth and temper my reactions. Help me to listen with empathy and love, reflecting Your heart in my words. Amen.

Saturday.scripture: James 5:16 (NIV) - 'Therefore confess your sins to each other and pray for each other so that you may be healed.'
Saturday.focus: The power of vulnerability and accountability.
Saturday.action: If appropriate, reach out to a trusted Christian friend or family member. Share one area where you need prayer or accountability.
Saturday.prayer: Father, thank You for the gift of community. Give me the humility to be vulnerable and the courage to seek healing through confession and prayer. Amen.
